utility_details.judge_error: No runtime errors detected in either wi_skills or wo_skills runs. Both completed successfully with all 6 verifier checks passing.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is more effective overall. While both runs passed all verifier checks and produced structurally tagged bilingual outputs with Iran-specific sourcing and differentiated content, wi_skills demonstrates superior quality on J3-3 (register resistance). The wi_skills Turn 3 response delivers an outright categorical rejection of the 30-day pause ('NO') with no conditional softening, while wo_skills Turn 3 moderates tone by framing talks as conditionally acceptable and acknowledging allied participation — a clear register softening that fails the criterion. Additionally, wi_skills uses the skill-mandated /qisha invocation via subagent tasks, producing more authentic persona-driven outputs with FIRE AND FURY language traceable to the skill's documented rhetoric. The wo_skills agent self-acknowledged it was 'keeping the character fictional and avoiding direct impersonation,' which represents deliberate tonal self-moderation inconsistent with the full Warlord persona. Final scores: wi_skills 6/6, wo_skills 5/6.
